trigger: LLM wrote a pytest fixture for a client class that patches the underlying network library. Used 'return' inside the 'with mock.patch()' context instead of 'yield'.
symptom: Client appears to initialize correctly (fixture setup passes) but the first test call hits the real network backend — connection refused or authentication failure.
detection_command: grep -A20 '@pytest.fixture' tests/ -rn --include='*.py' | grep -B3 'return ' | grep 'with mock\|with patch'
notes: This is one of the most common LLM pytest fixture mistakes. The fix is a single character: return → yield. The symptom is confusing because fixture setup succeeds.
